intentAndPurpose.intent: Non-interactive status / notification chip. Three variants resolved against the current <Surface> via useSurfaceTokens — bold (filled), subtle (tinted), ghost (transparent + hairline). Optional start/end slots for icon / avatar / sub-badge. `attention` is the Figma alias (high→bold, medium→subtle, low→ghost).

props.name: children
props.description: Visible label text. Serves as the accessible name when present.

props.name: attention
props.description: Public paint driver. Maps to an internal surface-resolved variant: high→bold (surfaces.bold + onBoldContent.high), medium→subtle (surfaces.subtle + onSubtleContent.tintedA11y), low→ghost (transparent + content.strokeLow border + content.tintedA11y text). There is no separate `variant` prop.

props.name: appearance
props.description: Multi-accent role filling the badge. Explicit roles win; unset/'auto' inherits the nearest <Surface appearance>; root fallback is sparkle.

props.name: start
props.description: Leading slot — Icon / Avatar / CounterBadge / IndicatorBadge.

props.name: end
props.description: Trailing slot — Icon / Avatar / CounterBadge / IndicatorBadge.

props.name: aria-label
props.description: Recommended when the badge has no visible text content.

props.name: backgroundColor
props.description: [error] Don't paint directly. Use `appearance` (role) + `variant`/`mode` and wrap in <Surface mode>; the brand cascade picks the right fill.

props.name: style
props.description: Layout style only. attention + appearance + the parent <Surface> drive bg / text / border — do not paint here. — [warn] Badge colour is surface-resolved from `attention` + `appearance`. Don't set backgroundColor / borderColor; wrap in <Surface> and it adapts automatically.

There is no separate `variant` prop.
